summary: Revamp the Indian Garden Chicago restaurant website (http://www.indiangardenchicago.com/) into a modern, visually stunning Indian-themed React website featuring rich cultural aesthetics, menu showcase, restaurant info, and warm modern design inspired by Indian art and colors.
imageRequirements:
## Required Images
- Ornate golden mandala pattern on a deep saffron-to-crimson gradient background, suitable for a full-screen hero section (hero-mandala-bg.dim_1920x1080.png)
- Vibrant overhead flat-lay of colorful Indian dishes — curries, naan, biryani, and chutneys arranged on a dark wooden surface with marigold flowers as garnish (food-spread-banner.dim_1400x600.png)
- Warm-toned illustration of an elegant Indian restaurant interior with ornate lanterns, rich tapestry walls, and candlelit tables (restaurant-ambiance.dim_1200x800.png)
- Circular Indian Garden restaurant logo badge with paisley border in gold and saffron on ivory background (restaurant-logo.dim_300x300.png)

existing_features.title: Create a visually rich homepage with a full-screen hero section featuring the restaurant name 'Indian Garden', a tagline, and a call-to-action button ('View Menu' / 'Reserve a Table'). The hero should use warm Indian-inspired colors (saffron, turmeric gold, deep crimson, ivory) and decorative motifs (paisley, mandala patterns) rendered via CSS or SVG. Include a sticky navigation bar with links to Menu, About, and Contact sections.

Build a Menu section showcasing food categories (e.g., Appetizers, Breads, Vegetarian Entrees, Non-Vegetarian Entrees, Biryanis, Desserts, Drinks) sourced from the original site at http://www.indiangardenchicago.com/. Each menu item card should display the dish name, description, and price. Cards should use a warm card design with subtle gold borders and Indian-inspired typography.

Build an About / Story section describing Indian Garden Chicago's history and culinary philosophy. Include a warm narrative about authentic Indian cuisine, the restaurant's founding story, and its Chicago presence. Style with an elegant side-by-side layout using a decorative image placeholder and text.

Build a Contact & Info section displaying: restaurant address (68 W. Washington, Chicago, IL), phone number, operating hours for each day of the week, and an embedded Google Maps iframe for the location. Style with a warm Indian-themed card layout and decorative dividers.

Apply a cohesive Indian art-inspired visual theme site-wide. Use a warm color palette of saffron (#FF9933), turmeric gold (#D4A017), deep crimson (#8B0000), and ivory (#FFFAF0) as primary colors. Typography should use an elegant serif font for headings (e.g., Playfair Display) and a clean sans-serif for body text. Include decorative section dividers using mandala or floral SVG patterns, and subtle texture overlays to evoke Indian textile aesthetics.

Create a footer with the restaurant logo text, quick navigation links, social media placeholder icons, address, and a copyright line. Style with a deep crimson or dark saffron background with ivory text.

Generate the following static image assets for use in the website and place them in frontend/public/assets/generated: a decorative mandala hero background, a food spread banner, and a restaurant ambiance illustration.

new_feature_requests.summary: Modern Indian-themed homepage with rich cultural aesthetics and hero section
Menu showcase page displaying food items with Indian-inspired design
Overall Indian art and color theme applied site-wide

Create a backend actor in Motoko to store and serve restaurant data including: menu items (name, description, price, category), restaurant info (address, phone, hours), and a list of specialties or featured dishes. Expose query functions for retrieving this data.
